design.type_A: cross-keyword: Python keyword → different Python keyword
design.type_B: long-phrase: Python keyword → 3+ word natural language phrase
design.type_C: short-nonkw: Python keyword → short non-keyword token

rigor_notes.alias_generation: random (seeded; seed=42,43,44); no researcher selection bias
rigor_notes.judge: deterministic rule-based (alias presence + keyword leakage + AST parse)
rigor_notes.n_per_cell: 5 trials per config; n=30 configs per type
rigor_notes.statistical_test: Wilson CI (95%) per type reported in summary
rigor_notes.type_A_collision_warning: Type A cross-kw aliases may produce src-alias collisions (e.g., alias(return)=for while for is also a source token). Judge conservatively flags such outputs as violations; true compliance may be slightly higher than reported KLR.
